summary: The CI job failed while trying to push a branch to the vercel/next.js repository; git push was rejected with an HTTP 403 permission error for the nextjs-bot account.
root_cause: git push failed because GitHub returned a 403 permission denied error for the nextjs-bot account when attempting to push the branch.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: Shows the failing command was a git push of the update/react branch and that it exited with a failure code.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicitly states that the push was denied because the nextjs-bot account lacks permission.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: Confirms the HTTP 403 response from GitHub, indicating an authorization/permission issue rather than a network/transient error.
Shows where in the project's script the failing git push was invoked (sync-react.js), useful for locating the push logic and credentials handling.

suggested_fix: Ensure the credentials used by the workflow can push to vercel/next.js: grant push permission to the nextjs-bot account or update the workflow to use a token/credentials with repo write access (e.g., a PAT or a GITHUB_TOKEN with appropriate permissions), and confirm the git remote URL and auth are configured correctly before running git push.
